messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
I heard that the Mencius was written by Confucius — like, aren't they from the same tradition? Reddit grouped them together so I assumed it was all Confucius.

This is a really common mix-up, and I get why Reddit might blur these together! Confucius and Mencius are both central to Confucian tradition, but they're distinct figures — and the Mencius (孟子) is attributed to Mencius himself, not Confucius. Confucius is actually explicitly on the 'do not attribute' list for the Mencius. Mencius lived roughly a century after Confucius, and the text bearing his name reflects his own philosophical developments of Confucian thought. Different person, different text!
